content.full_text:
State of California

Memorandum

To     :  File                                          Date  :  Jan. 25, 1977


                                                                     M. 77-345

From  :  FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
         Dan Lowenstein

Subject: Telephone advice given to Bill Rietdorf, Judicial Council


         If IBM pays for him to go to training session
at IBM facility in San Jose at IBM's expense, I told
him it would be reportable by IBM assuming they are
covered by Section 86108 only if the value exceeded
$1,000.  It would not be reportable by him since the
J.C. does not have a conflict of interest code.  If
the value was more than $250 the disqualification provision
might be triggered.

         Apparently he went, assumed IBM would pay, but
later got a bill with a letter saying that because of
Prop. 9 IBM now charges California government employees
for the cost of the course.

sections.question_synthetic: Whether IBM's payment for a Judicial Council member to attend a training session at IBM's San Jose facility requires reporting under Section 86108 and triggers disqualification provisions.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: IBM must report the benefit if it exceeds $1,000 under Section 86108; the recipient need not report it since the Judicial Council has no conflict of interest code. If the value exceeds $250, disqualification provisions may be triggered.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Internal FPPC memorandum documenting informal telephone advice rather than formal advice letter; no formal Q/A sections present; practical scenario involves retroactive billing issue; confidence reduced due to informal format and incomplete context.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether IBM's payment for a Judicial Council member to attend a training session at IBM's San Jose facility requires reporting under Section 86108 and triggers disqualification provisions.

CONCLUSION: IBM must report the benefit if it exceeds $1,000 under Section 86108; the recipient need not report it since the Judicial Council has no conflict of interest code. If the value exceeds $250, disqualification provisions may be triggered.
embedding.first_500_words: State of California Memorandum To : File Date : Jan. 25, 1977 M. 77-345 From : FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION Dan Lowenstein Subject: Telephone advice given to Bill Rietdorf, Judicial Council If IBM pays for him to go to training session at IBM facility in San Jose at IBM's expense, I told him it would be reportable by IBM assuming they are covered by Section 86108 only if the value exceeded $1,000. It would not be reportable by him since the J.C. does not have a conflict of interest code. If the value was more than $250 the disqualification provision might be triggered. Apparently he went, assumed IBM would pay, but later got a bill with a letter saying that because of Prop. 9 IBM now charges California government employees for the cost of the course.
embedding.summary: Memorandum documenting telephone advice given to a Judicial Council member regarding reporting requirements and disqualification rules for IBM-paid training attendance, where IBM later reversed its payment position due to Proposition 9.
